title: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser
impact: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser.

updates.content:
Title: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser

User impact: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser.

More info: The managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser may display an error stating “Action Not Allowed” when trying to launch the Microsoft Intune web app.
 
Current status: We're investigating a potential issue with Microsoft Intune and checking for impact to your organization. We'll provide an update within 30 minutes.

Title: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser

User impact: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser.

More info: The managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser may display an error stating “Action Not Allowed” when trying to launch the Microsoft Intune web app.

Current status: We've identified that a code issue in the Mobile Application Management (MAM) SDK conflicts with a recently enabled account management feature, resulting in impact. We're creating a new build of the Microsoft Edge iOS browser that has this feature disabled to remediate impact.

Scope of impact: Any user attempting to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser may be impacted.

Root cause: A code issue in the MAM SDK conflicts with a recently enabled account management feature, resulting in impact.

Next update by: Wednesday, April 15, 2026, at 6:00 PM UTC

Title: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser

User impact: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser.

More info: The managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser may display an error stating “Action Not Allowed” when trying to launch the Microsoft Intune web app.

Current status: We're continuing to creating a new build of the Microsoft Edge iOS browser that has the account management feature disabled to remediate impact.

Scope of impact: Any user attempting to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser may be impacted.

Root cause: A code issue in the MAM SDK conflicts with a recently enabled account management feature, resulting in impact.

Next update by: Wednesday, April 15, 2026, at 8:30 PM UTC

Title: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser

User impact: Users may be unable to access the Microsoft Intune web app from a managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser.

More info: The managed Microsoft Edge iOS browser may display an error stating “Action Not Allowed” when trying to launch the Microsoft Intune web app.
 
Final status: The investigation is complete and we've determined the service is healthy. A service incident didn't actually occur. This communication will expire in 24 hours.
 
This is the final update for the event.
